steps.title: Recognize the discriminant condition for multiple solutions
steps.content:
For the quadratic equation $88x^2 + bx + c = 0$ to have more than one real solution, it must have two distinct real solutions. This happens when the discriminant is positive.

The discriminant is:
$$\Delta = b^2 - 4ac$$
We need: $\Delta > 0$

steps.title: Apply the discriminant inequality and solve for b in terms of c
steps.content:
Substitute $a = 88$ into the discriminant inequality:
$$b^2 - 4(88)c > 0$$
$$b^2 - 352c > 0$$
$$b^2 > 352c$$
Since $b$ is positive, we can take the square root of both sides without flipping the inequality:
$$b > \sqrt{352c}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
